mechanism_narrative: A sustained low level of the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly increasing present equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns show no significant positive correlation with FEDFUNDS levels.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate exceeding 8.0 percent signals severe labor market slack and depressed consumer spending, which compresses corporate profit margins and triggers negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 65% of triggered instances despite the high unemployment signal, or the conditional return distribution fails a one-sided t-test at p < 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in retail sales and housing starts confirm broad-based consumer demand expansion, driving higher corporate revenues and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict positive 60-day returns with a Rank IC > 0.04, or the interaction term loses statistical significance in out-of-sample regression.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the index of industrial production during a post-recession recovery phase reflects expanding manufacturing capacity utilization, creating short-term momentum that yields positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Positive INDPRO growth in recovery regimes yields negative 5-day forward returns in > 55% of observations, or the signal's predictive power vanishes when regime filters are applied.

mechanism_narrative: A consumer price index level above 225.0 indicates accelerating inflation that forces central banks to tighten policy, raising discount rates and compressing equity multiples to produce negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in > 60% of instances when CPIAUCSL exceeds 225.0, or the threshold effect shows no statistical edge over a linear specification in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained month-over-month increase in total nonfarm payrolls demonstrates robust labor market expansion, which supports wage growth and consumer spending, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: Positive PAYEMS growth fails to predict positive 60-day returns with a Rank IC > 0.03, or the signal reverses direction during periods of high inflation without regime adjustment.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent quarter-over-quarter growth in real GDP and retail sales confirms a synchronized consumption and production boom, which elevates earnings expectations and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The interaction between GDPC1 and RSAFS growth rates yields a Rank IC below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows, or the joint signal fails to outperform single-series baselines in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in industrial production during a late-cycle expansion signals capacity constraints and slowing manufacturing activity, triggering short-term risk-off flows and negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Negative INDPRO growth in late-cycle regimes produces positive 5-day forward returns in > 60% of cases, or the regime-conditional signal shows no predictive edge over unconditional models.
